channel.title: Poetry on Starky Ink
channel.description: Recent content in Poetry on Starky Ink

item.title: Tar
item.description: The tar seeps in through every pore And coats the skin I had before It starts as just a heavy thought But soon consumes what daylight brought I try to lift my weighted feet But tar has made the ground complete Each step becomes a battle waged Against the dark that has me caged My arms are pinned against my sides The blackness where my spirit hides It pulls me down with steady grip

item.title: Whispers of Verse
item.description: Whispers of Verse Poetry is the most intimate form of writing. It strips away the excess and leaves only the essential — the image, the emotion, the truth beneath the words. Finding Your Voice Every poet starts by imitating. You’ll write sonnets like Shakespeare, free verse like Whitman, concrete poetry like Apollinaire. This is necessary. This is how we learn. But eventually, you must find your voice. What images haunt you?
